alt_sources: world-bank-archives-iran (1956-1972) | iran-data-portal (2005-2014) | iran-data-portal (1966-2011) | iran-disasters-regional-series [DUPLICATE -- no new data, see notes] (2005-2014) | iran-data-portal (2005-2014)
notes: Male/female labor force participation rate (age 10+) and unemployment rate at 1956 and 1966 censuses plus a 1972 Plan Organization projection. WDI's SL.TLF.CACT family for Iran only starts 1990 -- this adds 34 years of pre-WDI depth. Complements (does not duplicate) iran_disasters_regional_series/employed_population_by_gender_1966_2011.csv (staged separately below), which starts a decade later at 1966; this row's 1956 data point is the earliest labor-force-by-sex figure in the whole project. The 1956/1966 sectoral (agriculture/industry/services) breakdown recorded alongside these same rows is NOT separately registered here -- it duplicates, at coarser 2-year resolution, the fuller 1956-2011 annual series staged as iran_disasters__employed_persons_by_sector_1956_2011 below; noted there as a cross-validation source instead of a standalone chart. || WDI's SL.TLF.CACT family for Iran starts 1990 and gives no urban/rural split at all -- this national-estimate series (from SCI, not modeled ILO estimates) adds the urban/rural breakdown WDI never carries, for years that otherwise overlap WDI's own coverage. || Annual headcounts by sex (women's employment share tracked from 13.3% in 1966). WDI's labor-force-by-sex indicators (SL.TLF.CACT.FE/MA.ZS, participation RATES not headcounts) start 1990 -- this extends 24 years earlier, though the unit differs (headcount vs. %). Complements iran_census__wb_archives_labor_force_by_sex_1956_1972 (staged above), which adds a single earlier 1956 data point from an independent WB-archives source. || FOLLOW-UP to registration_completeness_audit.csv's dedup note on the iran_data_portal__labor_force_participation_rate_urbanrural_2005_2014 row. Verified via a full row-by-row diff (all 90 rows: 10 years x 3 areas x 3 sexes, Iranian year 1384-1393 / gregorian 2005-2014) that data/processed/iran_disasters_regional_series/labor_force_participation_rate_2005_2014.csv is BYTE-IDENTICAL in every value to the already-registered data/processed/iran_data_portal_deep_series/labor_force_participation_rate_gender_urbanrural_2005_2014.csv (same SCI/Ministry of Cooperatives Labour and Social Welfare source, e.g. 41%/64.7%/17% national/men/women 2005-06 matches exactly) -- only the column labels differ (area='total' vs 'national', sex='Both'/'Men'/'Women' vs gender='both_genders'/'men'/'women') plus 3 added metadata columns (unit, source, country_iso3) carrying no new information. CORRECTION to the audit's earlier guess that this file might be 'missing 2014-2015': it is not -- both files cover the exact same span through Iranian year 1393 (2014-2015 gregorian), confirmed identical, no coverage gap either direction. Registered here as a DEDUP ANNOTATION rather than a genuine alt-source: this row exists so the file is no longer un-registered (closes the last known orphan from the audit), but should NOT be read as independent corroboration -- it is the same underlying SCI/MCLS numbers reformatted, not a second data collection. If this file is ever pruned as pure redundancy, no information is lost; the identical figures remain in iran_data_portal_deep_series. || Native SCI/MCLS-sourced participation rate with urban/rural x gender breakdown that WDI's modeled-ILO-estimate series does not have -- kept as an alt-source line on the same chart concept per this project's 'both sources, never pick a winner' rule. DEDUP NOTE from this audit: a content-duplicate unregistered file also exists at iran_disasters_regional_series/labor_force_participation_rate_2005_2014.csv (same values, e.g. 41%/64.7%/17% national/men/women 2005-06) -- that sibling file is itself NOT yet in CHART_REGISTRY.csv or any staging file, which is outside this audit's assigned folder list; flagged here for a future pass, not fixed in this one to avoid scope creep beyond the assigned candidate folders.

notes: Full IMF-GFS-style fiscal-operations statement: total revenue, current + capital expenditure, deficit, savings, and a FINANCING breakdown by source (net borrowing abroad / net borrowing from the banking system / nonbank holdings of government securities). The financing-by-source split has no equivalent in WDI's GC.* group at any year -- genuinely more granular than the net-lending/borrowing chart it is mapped to, not just an earlier data point. Also jointly extends wdi__GC.REV.XGRT (Total Revenues row) and wdi__GC.XPN.TOTL (Current+Capital Expenditure rows); one extends_chart_id per staging row, so those two are noted here rather than given separate rows since this is one integrated statement in the source. This is the only one of the Pahlavi expenditure tables with a CAPITAL expenditure line (others are current/ordinary-budget only) -- not directly comparable to the ministry-level current-only breakdowns without accounting for that scope difference (see README). || WDI's Iran GC.* government-finance series only starts 1972 (confirmed against macro_wdi.csv) -- this sits 18-21 years before WDI's own coverage begins. Covers monthly/annual budget-deficit citations, the IBRD settlement proposal's financial terms, and the full NIE-46 (Feb 1952) financial-snapshot table. Same underlying concept (government revenue/expenditure/deficit position) as the target chart, same pre-WDI-gap logic as the precedent pahlavi__actual_budget_revenues_1958_59 row.

alt_sources: pahlavi-era-primary-extraction (FY1958/59 (single year)) | pahlavi-era-primary-extraction (FY1955/56-1959/60) | pahlavi-era-primary-extraction (FY1962/63-1971/72 (incl. estimate/draft columns)) | majlis-historical-budget-laws (FY1341-1370 (1963-1992 Western fiscal-year-end; 16 discontinuous fiscal years, 21 with any figure)) | majlis-historical-budget-laws (FY1301 (1922/23)) | iran-plan-budget-org (FY1371-1401 (1992-2023 Western fiscal-year-end; 30 of 31 possible fiscal years, FY1395 confirmed missing)) | cia-iran-economy (1965/66-1971/72 (draft budget)) | imf-article-iv-iran-staff-reports (2013/14-2022/23 (incl. Fund staff projections from 2018/19 or 2020/21 onward -- see date_label '(proj.)'/'(est.)' suffix))
notes: Same pre-1972 WDI-gap logic as actual_budget_revenues_1958_59. Ministry-level granularity (Ministry of War, Education, Finance, Health, etc.) -- WDI's GC.XPN.TOTL is a single national aggregate with no ministry breakdown at any year, so this is also strictly more granular than the chart it extends, not just an earlier point on the same line. || Pre-1972 WDI-gap extension for government expenditure, same as the other Pahlavi expenditure tables. Distinctive extra angle no WDI chart carries at any year: BUDGETED figure side-by-side with ACTUAL figure by ministry, i.e. budget-execution variance -- a structurally different question ('did they spend what they planned') from a simple expenditure-level time series. Flagging this bonus dimension rather than treating the file as pure duplicate-of-level-series. || Fills the pre-1972 gap in WDI's Iran GC.XPN.TOTL coverage, ending exactly at WDI's own start year. CURRENT/ordinary-budget expenditure only (Defense & Security, General Services, Social & Economic Services, Interest Payments) -- narrower scope than pahlavi__central_govt_operations_summary_1962_73's Current+Capital split for the same overlapping years (1962-1972); the two are not directly comparable without accounting for that scope difference, per README. || Jointly extends wdi__GC.XPN.TOTL (expenditure_rials column, primary) and wdi__GC.REV.XGRT (revenue_rials column, co-extended -- both figures appear together in every row since Iran budget law traditionally sets revenue=expenditure by design pre-FY1355, see README). IMPORTANT CONCEPT CAVEAT: this is ENACTED-LAW (ex-ante) figures from the Majlis's own gazetted text, NOT audited outturns -- WDI's GC.* series and this project's own pahlavi_government_finance_series/ (World-Bank-sourced, 1962/63-1971/72) are outturn/actuals-based. The 1963-1972 span in this file OVERLAPS pahlavi_government_finance_series's government_revenue_summary_1962_72.csv and ordinary_budget_expenditure_1962_72.csv, but per majlis_budget_law_series/README.md ('this folder does not yet reconcile against pahlavi_government_finance_series'), NO cross-check between law-vs-actual figures has been performed -- flagging as an open reconciliation item, not resolved here. Also carries FY1358's law-vs-component-sum gap (22.86bn rial), FY1368/FY1370's component-sum-exceeds-total gaps (451bn / 1.24tn rial), and FY1344's deficit-consistency mismatch -- all preserved exactly as printed per README, never resolved to one number. || Earliest government-expenditure data point in this database's government-finance collection (1922/23, 50 years before WDI's Iran GC.* coverage begins) -- but flagged as NOT directly comparable in magnitude to any later Rial-denominated series: FY1301 figures are in TOMANS (Iran's currency until the 1932 conversion, 1 toman = 10 rials), transcribed in original denomination with no retroactive conversion applied. Also carries the README's documented raw-filename mislabeling: the 'foreign-ministry' and 'public-works-ministry' raw HTML filenames are SWAPPED relative to actual body-text content -- this processed CSV uses the CORRECT ministry attribution, raw files left untouched per bookkeeping policy. || Direct year-continuation of majlis__national_budget_totals_by_fiscal_year (which itself extends wdi__GC.XPN.TOTL/GC.REV.XGRT through FY1370) -- this chart picks up at exactly FY1371, one fiscal year after the Majlis series ends, from a separately-sourced raw folder (Iran Data Portal, not lamtakam.com). Same IMPORTANT CONCEPT CAVEAT applies: these are ENACTED-LAW (ex-ante) figures from the law's own single-article clause, NOT audited outturns -- WDI's GC.* series is outturn/actuals-based, so years where both exist should be read as two different vintages of the same fiscal year, not cross-validated or merged here. Jointly extends wdi__GC.XPN.TOTL (expenditure_rials, primary) and wdi__GC.REV.XGRT (revenue_rials, co-extended -- revenue=expenditure in every row, the law sets a balanced budget by design). Also carries internal Part A (general govt)/Part A1 (general revenue)/Part A2 (earmarked revenue)/Part B (state companies+banks) breakdown not present in WDI's single aggregate. STRUCTURAL FINDING: in every year both Part A and Part B were captured (~20 years), A+B numerically EXCEEDS the printed Grand Total -- confirmed as a persistent, unexplained feature of Iranian budget-law drafting (first flagged for FY1368/1370 in the Majlis series), not a transcription error; kept as printed throughout, never reconciled. Two fiscal years (FY1397, FY1398) carry TWO distinct figures each (bill-stage vs. final-enacted-law), both kept as separate rows in the underlying CSV, neither treated as authoritative. FY1395 is a confirmed missing source (no PDF exists in the raw folder for that year), not a legibility failure. Full caveats: data/processed/iran_plan_budget_org_series/README.md. || WDI's Iran GC.* coverage only starts 1972, so this predates it for all but the final year (confirmed against macro_wdi.csv). Covers Figure 28 (revenue by oil/nonoil/tax type, expenditure, deficit financing), Figure 29 (expenditure by function incl. defense -- finer than WDI's single aggregate, same precedent as the pahlavi ministry-level extends rows), and Figure 30 (Third vs. Fourth Development Plan investment by sector, which also predates and could alternately extend iranplanbudgetorg__five_year_plan_investment_ceilings [FY1368-1378/1990-2000] -- kept bundled here rather than split into a separate row since Figure 30 has no isolable category tag of its own). || HIGH-CONFIDENCE extends call: WDI's Iran GC.* government-finance series stops at 2009 (confirmed against macro_wdi.csv -- max year across all GC.* codes for IRN is 2009), so this table sits entirely in a confirmed, genuine post-2009 coverage gap. Also touches wdi__GC.REV.XGRT (revenue, incl. nontax/oil/tax sub-line-items) and wdi__GC.NLD.TOTL (net lending/borrowing) concepts. Line-item granularity (e.g. 'Revenue -- Nontax revenue -- Oil revenue') exceeds WDI's own aggregate on top of the pure time-gap.

notes: One of the longest continuous annual series in the whole project (55 years, national level, no urban/rural or sex split). WDI's SL.AGR.EMPL.ZS/SL.IND.EMPL.ZS/SL.SRV.EMPL.ZS (3-sector, modeled ILO estimates) start only 1991 for Iran -- this extends 35 years earlier with actual headcounts at much finer (13-sector) granularity; the 13-way split doesn't decompose cleanly onto WDI's 3 broad sectors, so treat the 'extends' relationship as topical (same underlying question) rather than a literal continuation of the same series. Cross-validated against a 1956/1966 3-sector citation series in iran_census_demographics_series/wb_archives_population_demographic_citations_1956_1982.csv (agriculture/industry/services %-of-active-population rows) -- percentages agree at those two overlapping years; that WB-archives citation was NOT given its own chart_registry row since it duplicates this fuller series at coarser resolution.

notes: Primary driver of extends status: the Liquidity(M2) level+growth columns (23/23 years, full coverage) extend PAST WDI's Iran FM.LBL.BMNY cutoff (2016) through FY1401 (2022/23) -- 6-7 additional years no WDI chart reaches for Iran. For the OVERLAPPING years (2001-2016) this is an independent CBI-sourced alt vintage of the same broad-money concept, not previously cross-checked against WDI's own FM.LBL.BMNY figures for those years (flagging as an open reconciliation item, not performed here). Monetary base column (19/23 growth-rate years, 5/23 level years) has NO direct WDI Iran analog at any year -- a genuinely new sub-component bundled into this same row rather than split out, given its partial/secondary coverage. CPI/inflation column (6/23 years) is REDUNDANT with WDI's already-FULL Iran FP.CPI.TOTL.ZG coverage (1960-2025, no gap) -- explicitly NOT counted as an extension, included in this row only for completeness/traceability. Also carries README's documented FY1396 raw-filename Western-year mislabeling and the FY1383 inter-report revision example (both flagged, neither resolved to one number). || Native CBI Annual Review (English edition) fiscal-year series -- full 23/23-year Liquidity(M2) level + growth coverage (WDI's Broad Money for Iran runs only 1960-2016 and stops before this file's 2017-2023 tail), plus bundled monetary-base growth (19/23 yrs), monetary-base level (5/23 yrs), and CPI/inflation growth (6/23 yrs) -- all extracted directly from each year's own report narrative, never a later year's retrospective restatement. Retrieved via Wayback (cbi.ir itself is F5/TSPD bot-blocked from this project's IP). One raw-filename mislabeling (cbi_annual_review_1396_2016-17.pdf is actually FY1396=2017/18) is documented in the folder README and NOT corrected in the raw filename per bookkeeping.md immutability rule -- this project's own fiscal_year_ah field is authoritative. Units: billion rials FY1379-1393, converted from trillion rials (x1000) FY1394-1401 for consistency. || WDI's Iran FM.* monetary coverage stops at 2016 (confirmed against macro_wdi.csv), so the 2018 report's years extending to 2022/23 are a genuine gap-fill beyond that. The 2015 report's years (mostly <=2016) substantially duplicate/corroborate existing WDI FM coverage -- net effect is 'extends' given the real 2017-2022 tail, plus the table's central-bank-vs-deposit-money-bank line-item detail exceeds WDI's single aggregate figure regardless of year.

title: Population, total
title_fa: جمعیت، کل
alt_sources: iranica-online (1956-1998) | sci-iran-census (2006-2011) | sci-iran-census (2011) | sci-iran-census (1956-2016) | sci-iran-census (2011-2016) | world-bank-archives-iran (1956-1972) | sci-iran-census (2011) | sci-iran-census (2016) | sci-iran-census (2016) | iran-data-portal (2006) | pahlavi-era-primary-extraction (1900 (rough estimate) vs. 2006) | imf-article-iv-iran-staff-reports (2015/16-2022/23)
notes: Headline population totals at each of the 8 national censuses (1956-1996) plus 1995 mid-decade estimate. Cross-validates to the exact digit against un-demographic-yearbook-iran and wb_archives_population_demographic_citations_1956_1982.csv (1956: 18,954,704; 1976: 33,708,744; 1986: 49,445,010 -- three independent sources agree exactly per this folder's own README). WDI's SP.POP.TOTL for Iran only starts 1960, so 1956 census total is a genuine pre-WDI depth extension; later years are corroborating alt-source detail (census methodology, staff counts, under-enumeration rates) not carried by WDI at all. || National 2006/2011 population + growth rate (Table 1) and the same broken out for all 31 provinces (Table 9). WDI's SP.POP.TOTL has no provincial breakdown at all for Iran -- province-level granularity is the genuine value-add here; the national totals themselves duplicate WDI's existing 2006/2011 coverage. || 18 five-year age bands, both-sexes total, single cross-section. WDI carries the same concept as many separate single-band indicators (SP.POP.0004.MA/FE.5Y etc., each its own chart_id) -- no single WDI chart_id bundles the full age pyramid the way this census table does, so this is registered as one extension of the broad population chart rather than 18 separate rows. || National population/household/sex-ratio trend across 5 census years, plus absolute and annualized population increase for each intercensal period back to 1956. WDI's SP.POP.TOTL already covers 1960 onward at national level; the households/sex-ratio companion series and the pre-1960-anchored 1956 intercensal-increase figure are the genuine additions. || Province-level growth rate for the 2011-2016 intercensal period, complementing the iran_census__population_growth_national_province_2006_2011 row above (2006-2011 period) with the following intercensal window from the same bulletin series. || Province/governorate-level population, reshaped wide-to-long from WB 1974 Statistical Appendix Table 1.1. WDI has no province breakdown ever for Iran, and the 1956/1966/1967 points predate WDI's 1960 start -- genuine pre-WDI, sub-national depth extension. Source footnote: province totals don't sum exactly to the national total (independent province-by-province projection basis), preserved as printed. || 7,548-row full cross-tabulation (31 provinces x age bands x sex x Total/Urban/Rural/Unsettled residence), extracted from 31 separate provincial source files. Sums reconcile exactly to the national 75,149,669 total. This is the single most granular demographic cross-section in the whole project; WDI has no province-level data for Iran at any granularity, so this is a major extension of the population chart family rather than any single existing WDI chart_id. || 1,440-row province x age-band x sex cross-tabulation, the 2016-census companion to the 2011 file above (no residence/urban-rural split at this vintage). Includes the corrected Kerman/Kermanshah mislabeling fix documented in this folder's README (raw source itself labeled both provinces' rows 'Kermanshah'; fixed on the way in per bookkeeping.md, raw file left untouched). || National + 31-province population, households, sex split, and urban/settled-rural/unsettled breakdown. Sums reconcile exactly to national totals (79,926,270 persons; 24,196,035 households). Deliberately excludes ~1,800 rows of shahrestan (sub-province) detail present in the raw file (kept for a future deeper pass, per README). || All 30 provinces + national, sums reconcile exactly to the national 2006 total (70,495,782), independently cross-checked against iran_census_demographics_series/national_and_provincial_summary_2011_census.csv Table 1. WDI has no province or settlement-type breakdown for Iran. || Bundles 7 different macro/demographic concepts as a single two-point (1900, 2006) bookend table (Esfahani & Pesaran 2008, Table 1); this row's single extends_chart_id captures Population (wdi__SP.POP) as primary, but the file equally extends: wdi__NY.GDP.PCAP (per-capita income), wdi__SP.DYN.LE00 (life expectancy), wdi__SE.ADT.LITR (literacy rate, pop. 15+), wdi__SP.URB.TOTL.IN (urbanization rate), wdi__NV.AGR.TOTL (agriculture share of GDP), and wdi__NE.TRD.GNFS (trade/GDP ratio) -- all confirmed present in CHART_REGISTRY.csv. The unique contribution is the single 1900 anchor point, decades before WDI/Maddison's own Iran coverage begins for most of these series (WDI Population/GDP-per-capita start 1960). 1900 values are explicitly labeled rough estimates by the source (several carry a '?' or '<' marker, preserved in notes) -- treat as order-of-magnitude anchors, not precise data points. See pahlavi__century_indicators_world_rank_1900_2006 (this file's other row) for the world-rank sub-statistics, split out as status=new. || WDI's population series for Iran is dense and continuous, so this substantially duplicates/corroborates existing coverage for the overlap years; kept as extends given the straightforward concept match, though the incremental value is mostly the Fund's specific sourcing trail rather than new data.

UNIT-BASIS CAVEAT: this file reports raw bank/branch COUNTS and branches-per-MILLION-population, not WDI's branches-per-100,000-adults ratio -- not a drop-in splice, needs a unit-basis reconciliation before combining on one chart, not attempted here. SPARSE, not continuous (9 points across 97 years) -- do not interpolate between points without labeling gaps. Brackets a real discontinuity, not a data gap: the 1977->1982 span captures the 1979 bank-nationalization shock (36 banks/8,275 branches -> 9 banks/6,581 branches) -- see banking_hist__nationalization_1979_events for the paired event log. Source (Encyclopaedia Iranica) itself flags a confirmed digitization gap for 6 referenced-but-never-digitized data tables (Tables 25-30) -- a genuine source-side gap per README, not a search failure.

alt_sources: pahlavi-era-primary-extraction (FY1963/64-1969/70 (+ 7-year CAGR summary rows)) | cia-iran-economy (1965/66-1971/72) | imf-article-iv-iran-staff-reports (2013/14-2022/23)
notes: WDI's Iran balance-of-payments series (BN.CAB.XOKA.CD, BX.GSR.TOTL.CD, BM.GSR.TOTL.CD) only start 1976 -- this table covers 1963/64-1969/70, well before that gap. Covers exports (oil vs non-oil split), imports (goods vs services), current account, capital account, and monetary movements -- broader than just the current-account-balance chart it is mapped to; also touches BX.GSR.TOTL/BM.GSR.TOTL concepts. One illegible cell (1964/65 errors-and-omissions) left blank per no-fabrication rule -- see README caveats before charting. || HIGH VALUE: Figure 35's complete BOP table. WDI's Iran BN.* balance-of-payments coverage doesn't start until 1976 (confirmed against macro_wdi.csv), so this entire table predates WDI's Iran BOP coverage by up to 11 years -- a genuine gap-filler, alongside the FRUS 1951-54 government-finance/FX extends rows above, for the project's overall pre-WDI-era BOP coverage. || MIXED, net extends: WDI's Iran current-account-balance series (BN.CAB.XOKA) stops at 2000 (confirmed against macro_wdi.csv), so this is a genuine ~13-22 year gap-filler for the net current-account concept. However WDI's gross goods/services trade flows (BX.*/BM.* prefixes) DO have near-continuous Iran coverage through 2024 (also confirmed), so this table's goods/services export and import sub-lines substantially duplicate/corroborate existing WDI series for overlapping years rather than filling a gap. Targeted the net-CAB chart since that concept has the larger genuine gap.

title: Citrus Fruit, Total
title_fa: تولید، سطح زیر کشت و عملکرد مرکبات، جمع کل

title: Maritime Profile Key Figures (fleet, port calls, seafarers), Comparator Countries

title: Five-Year Development Plan Headline GDP Growth Targets (Plans 2, 6, 7)
title_fa: اهداف اصلی رشد تولید ناخالص داخلی در برنامه‌های توسعه پنج‌ساله (برنامه‌های دوم، ششم و هفتم)
time_range: FY1374-1406 (1995/96-2027/28, non-continuous: Plan 2 [1374-78], Plan 6 [1396-1400], Plan 7 BILL [1402-1406]; Plans 3-5 not located)
notes: A POLICY-TARGET series, not a measured-outcome series -- structurally distinct from every GDP-growth chart already in the registry (WDI, Maddison, OWID all measure realized/estimated growth; this measures what the law/bill SET AS THE TARGET for the coming five years). No existing chart_id in the registry covers Iranian development-plan targets at all. Plan 2 (FY1374-78) targets 5.1% avg annual GDP growth (constant 1361 prices); Plan 6 (FY1396-1400) and Plan 7 (BILL, FY1402-1406, not yet enacted at collection time) both target 8% avg annual growth, alongside a target Gini coefficient of 0.34 by Plan 6's final year -- an unusual instance of an explicit inequality target inside a growth-plan law. Plans 1, 3, 4, 5's headline growth targets were NOT located within this extraction pass (Plan 1 delegates to an unopened appendix; Plans 3-5 are organized as policy-reform chapters without a compact targets clause) -- genuine gaps, flagged not filled. A natural future chart pairing: overlay this against WDI's actual Iran GDP-growth series (wdi__NY.GDP.MKTP.KD.ZG or equivalent) to visualize target-vs-outcome, once that overlay work is done -- not attempted here, this staging entry is data landing only.

title: Five-Year Development Plan Sectoral Value-Added & Employment Growth Targets (Plans 6-7)
title_fa: اهداف رشد ارزش‌افزوده بخشی و اشتغال در برنامه‌های توسعه پنج‌ساله (برنامه‌های ششم و هفتم)
notes: 9-sector (agriculture, oil, mining, industry, water/power/gas, construction, transport/storage, communications, other services) average-annual value-added growth AND employment growth targets, Plan 6 (enacted law) plus Plan 7's comparable sector-growth targets (BILL). No existing chart_id covers sector-level GROWTH TARGETS for Iran (WDI's NV.* sector value-added series are realized levels, not targets). Widest target seen: Communications 19.4% avg annual value-added growth in Plan 6, by far the highest of any sector, consistent with Iran's stated post-JCPOA push to diversify from oil. Plan 7's bill omits a directly-labeled 'other services'-style residual breakdown identical to Plan 6's table structure in the sampled pages -- treat the two plans' sector lists as close but not guaranteed byte-identical categories.

title: Five-Year Development Plan Investment & Credit Ceilings by Year (Plans 1-2)
title_fa: سقف سرمایه‌گذاری و اعتبارات در برنامه‌های توسعه پنج‌ساله به تفکیک سال (برنامه‌های اول و دوم)
notes: Annual investment-credit / bank-facility ceilings stated directly in each plan's own ماده واحده clause -- a distinct policy-instrument concept (a legislated spending CEILING by year) rather than a realized-spending level, so classified new rather than as an extension of any existing investment/capital-formation chart. Plan 1's figures are total 5-year program envelopes split by funding source (general revenue vs. state-company internal resources vs. current/operating spending) plus a separate annual bank-facility ceiling table (FY1368: 1,210bn rials through FY1371: 623bn rials, FY1372 blank in source). Plan 2's figures are a single annual ceiling table from general-revenue budget (FY1374: 36,394bn rials rising to FY1378: 60,825bn rials) -- a much larger nominal scale than Plan 1's five years earlier, consistent with this database's broader finding of rapid nominal budget growth across the 1990s (see iranplanbudgetorg__annual_budget_law_totals for the same-era whole-country totals).

title: Iran Air Founding, Fleet Size, Workforce & Major Accidents (1952-1988)
title_fa: تأسیس ایران‌ایر، اندازه ناوگان، نیروی کار و سوانح عمده (1952-1988)
notes: No existing chart tracks airline fleet size, workforce, or aviation accidents for Iran. Workforce grew 700 (1962) to 12,000+ (1978/79); fleet reached 35 all-jet aircraft carrying ~5 million passengers/year by approx. 1978 (year is imprecise in source, flagged as such); starkest single data point is the 1988 Iran Air Flight 655 shootdown (290 deaths). Encyclopaedia Iranica narrative source, retrieved via interactive browser tool (iranicaonline.org blocks curl/WebFetch, HTTP 403).

title: Employment by Sector, 14-Sector Breakdown, Absolute Headcounts (1956-2011)
title_fa: اشتغال به تفکیک بخش، تجزیه 14 بخشی، تعداد مطلق شاغلان (1956-2011)
notes: Statistical Centre of Iran, via Iran Data Portal. Absolute headcount levels across 14 sectors (agriculture, oil, mining, industry, construction, trade, services split multiple ways, uncategorized) -- structurally distinct from WDI's SL.AGR/IND/SRV.EMPL.ZS (which are % shares, only 3 sectors, and only start 1991 for Iran as modeled ILO estimates). One of the two deepest continuous Iran-specific economic series in the database outside Maddison's reconstruction (per folder README), spanning nearly the entire Second Pahlavi era plus the full Islamic Republic period unbroken. Kept as its own chart rather than an alt-source footnote given this depth and structural distinctness.

title: 1950s-60s Land Reform Baseline: Motheral Report & Village Redistribution
title_fa: خط مبنای اصلاحات ارضی دهه‌های 1950 و 1960: گزارش Motheral و بازتوزیع روستایی
notes: Up to 60% of Iran's population as tenant farmers into the early 1960s (cited to Craig 1978); the Shah's 1951 firman redistributing 2,100 of his own villages; the 1952 Point Four program's initial 3,000-of-50,000-village scope; Motheral's proposed 1958-1962 redistribution timeline for 15,000+ villages; the 1958 founding of IDBI and 1958-1960 establishment of the Central Bank of Iran; the Shah's June 1963 White Revolution framing ('emancipating fifteen million Iranians'). Same secondary-source caveat as the row above (2025 PhD research report, one citation-hop removed) -- exactly the land-reform-baseline angle the task brief flagged as not otherwise tracked by this project's machine-readable sources.
